fundamentals.name: MP Materials Corp.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MP Materials Corp., together with its subsidiaries, produces rare earth materials in the Western Hemisphere. It operates in two segments, Materials and Magnetics. The Materials segment owns and operates the Mountain Pass Rare Earth Mine and Processing facility located near Mountain Pass, San Bernardino County, California. The Magnetics segment produces magnetic precursor products, including NdPr metal; and manufactures NdFeB permanent magnets. MP Materials Corp. was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Las Vegas, Nevada.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but unprofitable, with a high valuation and negative cash flow.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 118.6% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS beat estimate by 174.6%.
Analyst consensus is strong buy with a mean target of $80.40.
Current ratio 7.18 and quick ratio 6.51 indicate strong short-term liquidity.
negatives: Operating margin -7.9% and profit margin -20.5% indicate unprofitability.
Return on equity -4.2% and return on assets -2.1% show poor capital efficiency.
Free cash flow (TTM) is negative $-174.84M, suggesting cash burn.
Forward P/E of 56.35 is very high, especially for an unprofitable company.
Beta of 1.91 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: MP Materials has seen its stock price decline despite a recent partnership with Apple, with articles highlighting valuation concerns, insider selling, and geopolitical factors. While the company reported a significant beat on its last earnings per share, its operating and profit margins remain negative, and its stock is trading at a high forward P/E. The recent price action has been described as strange, with some analyses suggesting the stock is overvalued despite a strong buy consensus from analysts.
